skill.name: writing-docs
skill.description: Helps create and improve technical documentation for code, APIs, README files, docstrings, and project guides.
skill.summary: Write clear technical documentation for code, APIs, README files, and project guides.
skill.author: C0ntr0lledCha0s

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many external command and weak cryptography patterns, but review shows these are Markdown documentation templates and placeholder examples. No executable scripts, prompt injection attempts, credential access, or data exfiltration behavior were found. The skill has low risk because it legitimately requests file read and edit tools for documentation work.

low_findings.title: Filesystem Editing Capability
low_findings.description: The skill declares Read, Write, Edit, Glob, and Grep tools. This is expected for documentation updates, but users should review file changes before accepting them.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The allowed-tools declaration directly confirms file access. The capability matches the stated documentation purpose and does not show malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Dismissed External Command Matches in Documentation Examples
low_findings.description: Static external command matches occur inside fenced examples, comments, and README template text. They are sample documentation content, not commands executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown code fences or documentation examples. The skill does not declare shell execution tools or include runnable script files.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Placeholder URL in README Template
low_findings.description: The only hardcoded URL is a placeholder GitHub clone URL in a README template. It is documentation text and not an outbound network request.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a fenced Markdown README example. No network-capable tool or executable code uses it.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Weak Cryptography Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts point to ordinary words, placeholders, headings, and Markdown table text. No cryptographic API or algorithm implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited lines shows prose and template placeholders, not MD5, SHA-1, DES, or other weak algorithm use.

low_findings.title: Dismissed System Reconnaissance Keyword Matches
low_findings.description: The reconnaissance alerts point to documentation prose and identifier examples. No command gathers host, process, network, or environment information.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are normal documentation language such as jargon guidance, error names, and parameter descriptions. No reconnaissance behavior is present.

content.value_statement: Technical documentation is often incomplete, inconsistent, or hard to scan. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured docs for code, APIs, and projects.
actual_capabilities: Drafts README sections with installation, usage, API reference, configuration, and contributing guidance.
Creates language-aware docstrings for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, and Rust examples.
Adds structured documentation for functions, classes, modules, callbacks, options objects, and generic types.
Applies documentation principles for clarity, scannability, examples, audience fit, and quality review.
Supports documentation updates through file reading, editing, searching, and writing tools.
limitations: It does not execute tests or verify that example commands work unless another tool is used.
It depends on available source code and project context for accurate API details.
It may need human review for product positioning, legal wording, or public release notes.
It provides templates and guidance, not a complete documentation hosting system.

use_cases.title: Document a New Library
use_cases.description: Create a README, usage examples, and API reference for a small package before release.

use_cases.title: Improve Inline Documentation
use_cases.description: Add clear docstrings and comments to functions, classes, and modules in an existing codebase.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Docs
use_cases.description: Apply consistent headings, parameter descriptions, examples, and quality checks across project documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Document One Function
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner task for a small code unit.
prompt_templates.prompt: Document this function using the style already used in this file. Include parameters, return value, errors, and one practical example.

prompt_templates.title: Create a README
prompt_templates.scenario: Project-level documentation for a new repository.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a README for this project. Include purpose, features, installation, quick start, usage, configuration, API overview, contributing, and license sections.

prompt_templates.title: Refresh API Documentation
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate task for a public API surface.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review the exported API in this package and update the documentation. Keep examples concise and note errors, defaults, and edge cases.

prompt_templates.title: Build a Documentation Plan
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced task for a larger codebase.
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze the documentation gaps in this repository and propose an update plan. Prioritize user-facing docs, API references, examples, and maintenance checks.

output_examples.input: A function has parameters but no docstring.
output_examples.output: A concise docstring that explains purpose, parameters, return value, possible errors, and one usage example.

output_examples.input: A repository has source files but no README.
output_examples.output: A structured README with project summary, installation steps, quick start, usage notes, configuration details, and contribution guidance.

output_examples.input: An API reference is missing defaults and error behavior.
output_examples.output: Updated API documentation with parameter types, optional defaults, return behavior, raised errors, and related links.

best_practices: Provide the relevant source files and existing documentation style before asking for updates.
Ask for examples that match real project behavior and tested workflows.
Review generated documentation for accuracy, product tone, and version-specific details.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for generic docs without providing code, audience, or project context.
Do not publish generated install commands before confirming package names and supported versions.
Do not use documentation to hide unclear behavior, security assumptions, or known limitations.

faq.question: What types of documentation can this skill write?
faq.answer: It can write README files, docstrings, API references, code comments, module docs, and documentation quality checklists.

faq.question: Which programming languages are covered by the templates?
faq.answer: The skill includes examples for JavaScript, TypeScript, Python, Go, and Rust documentation styles.

faq.question: Can it update files directly?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill declares read, write, edit, glob, and grep tools so it can inspect and update documentation files.

faq.question: Does it verify that generated examples run correctly?
faq.answer: Not by itself. Ask the assistant to run tests or commands separately when example accuracy matters.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found low risk and no malicious behavior, but file edits should still be reviewed.

faq.question: How should I get the best results?
faq.answer: Provide source code, target audience, existing style rules, and any required sections before requesting documentation.
